programme.title: On the Case: One Month of Terror
programme.desc: After abducting a 17 year old girl in front of her home her kidnapper taunts the family with phone calls and disturbing letters. But a tiny mistake will eventually lead police to his door.

programme.title: On the Case: A Complicated Relationship
programme.desc: A beloved mother of four disappears just days after her stepdaughter vanished. Police must investigate to find out if their complicated relationship led to tragedy.

programme.title: On the Case: Unanswered Questions
programme.desc: Patti Berry disappears on the way to pick up her child at the babysitter's, then her body is found in a secluded wooded area not far from her abandoned blood-soaked car.

programme.title: On the Case: Gone in an Instant
programme.desc: In Lenexa, Kansas, young women disappear from their apartments as police piece together evidence showing the terrifying crimes are connected.

programme.title: On the Case: A Test of Patience
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of a teenager torments a small midwestern city for decades until a relentless team of investigators finally finds the clue that breaks the case wide open.

programme.title: On the Case: Scent of a Killer
programme.desc: The investigation into Sandra Iverson's stabbing death goes cold until a police canine team uncovers a trail leading them to an unlikely young suspect.

programme.title: On the Case: Shortcut to Murder
programme.desc: Paula Zahn goes beyond the headlines in search of fascinating mysteries from within America's justice system. A shortcut home from school causes a 14-year-old girl to cross paths with a murderer.

programme.title: On the Case: A Call for Justice
programme.desc: After a man confesses to a terrible double homicide, police must determine whether he actually committed the crime.

programme.title: On the Case: No One To Trust
programme.desc: A beautiful young girl vanishes on a desolate stretch of highway. Could the man in charge of the investigation be responsible?

programme.title: On the Case: Follow the Footprints
programme.desc: When a young girl's lifeless body turns up on the shore of the Feather River, police hope that following a trail of footsteps and discarded articles of clothing will lead them to her killer.

programme.title: On the Case: The Long Path to Justice
programme.desc: A couple's daily walk ends in murder and a cloud of suspicion descends on the victim's family.

programme.title: On the Case: A Dream Shattered
programme.desc: When a young teacher fails to show up for work, her principal goes looking for her at her home, where he makes a shocking discovery. The case would go cold until DNA technology helps police track down her killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Desperate Cry
programme.desc: A mother's desperate cry for help alerts neighbors and leads to a terrifying discovery.

programme.title: On the Case: Murder At Sunset- Part 1
programme.desc: A mother and two daughters are discovered floating in Tampa Bay after a brutal triple homicide. Police must rely on a victim who survived a similar attack to bring the killer to justice in this special two-hour On The Case.

programme.title: On the Case: Stolen Dreams
programme.desc: John Greathouse finds his stepdaughter, Kelli Bailey, murdered in her bedroom; investigators find a video tape and a dead man's forged check and hope these clues will be the keys to bringing Bailey's cold-blooded killer to justice.

programme.title: On the Case: A Mystery on Cherry Hill
programme.desc: When a beautiful young woman vanishes without a trace, it may take another tragedy before police will be able to solve the mystery of her disappearance.

programme.title: On the Case: Twisted Obsession
programme.desc: When a beautiful young woman is found murdered out in the New Mexico wilderness, police must unravel her killer's twisted obsession in order to solve the crime.

programme.title: On the Case: Fatal Errand
programme.desc: When a young woman vanishes after stepping out to buy salad dressing, police try to provide answers to her fiancé and family.

programme.title: On the Case: Race To Judgment
programme.desc: A late-night crash claims the lives of four girls. Texas community is torn apart in the search for justice.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into what he believes to be a vampire, and must keep away from his new bride (Shae D'Lynn) to prevent himself from biting her. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the life of a 1950's college student who is posing as a superhero.
programme.sub-title: Return of the Evil Leaper - October 8, 1956

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Alia's (Renee Coleman) arrival precipitates a ``lover's leap'' with Sam (Scott Bakula) that lands them in a women's prison where Zoey (Carolyn Seymour) attempts to kill them.
programme.sub-title: Revenge of the Evil Leaper - September 16, 1987

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes Marilyn Monroe's (Susan Griffiths) chauffeur to keep her from committing suicide, but Marilyn's new secretary (Liz Vassey) may prevent Sam's rescue. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes a rugged bear-like man who inhabits a mountainside forest with a troubled disabled Vietnam buddy (Sean Gregory). With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: In an unprecedented leap, Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes his great-grandfather, a Civil War captain, who must make his grandmother (Kate McNeil) fall in love with him in order to secure his future. With Dean Stockwell.
programme.sub-title: The Leap Between the States - September 20, 1862

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes Elvis only days before he is discovered and risks his promising career when he helps a young woman (Mary Elizabeth McGlynn) escape from an abusive fiance (John Scott Clough). With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into himself in a tavern in the middle of a coal-mining town filled with familiar faces; Sam (Scott Bakula) gets an opportunity to change Al's (Dean Stockwell) life.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: On the premiere episode of "Quantum Leap," Sam Beckett wakes up in 1956, disoriented, suffering from amnesia, but he has the awful realization that he does not belong there... Part 1 of 2.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam takes part in the Quantum Leap experiment which lands him in 1956 as a test pilot. With the help of his holographic "Observer", Sam learns that his fate is to "quantum leap" through time with the hope of returning to his own present. Part 2 of 2.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the persona of a womanizing college professor in 1972, encounters his lost love (Teri Hatcher) and attempts to reshape the past. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) lands in 1974 as a young boxer caught between fight-fixing gangsters and hopeful nuns who now own his contract. With Dean Stockwell. Guest stars Guy Stockwell and Teri Copley.
programme.sub-title: The Right Hand of God - October 24, 1974

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: When Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes an attractive female secretary in 1961, he must deal with rampant sexism and his employer's lecherous advances. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) finds himself on a Texas ranch in 1956 as a submissive veterinarian in love with a tough rancher (Kari Lizer). With Dean Stockwell.
programme.sub-title: How the Tess Was Won - September 28, 1956

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes a hit man in 1965 whose romance with the godfather's (Michael Genovese) girlfriend (Terri Garber) places him in danger. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) experiences racism firsthand when he leaps into the body of an elderly black man in the pre-civil rights South of 1955. Guest star: Susan French. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the life of a teen-age hot-rodder who must stop his sister from marrying a wealthy bully. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes a small-time detective in 1953 who is suspected of murdering his partner. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sam (Scott Bakula) jumps into the life of a ``ghostbuster'' who has been asked to silence the other-world murmurings of a dead romance novelist. Deborah Pratt guest stars. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Killer Britain
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan is joined by former Met detective Clive Driscoll to look back at the case of Harry Jarvis, the cheating husband who kept his mistress in the shed while plotting to murder his wife.

programme.title: Killer Town
programme.desc: Keith Black, a taekwondo instructor, disappears without a trace in West Kelowna, a quaint community in British Columbia. His family notifies the authorities. However, investigations into the disappearance slow down due to a lack of evidence.

programme.title: Killer Town
programme.desc: In a tiny hamlet just outside the town of Bonnyville, Alberta, Canada, 58-year-old Doreen Bradley, owner and operator of a successful fast food restaurant, is found murdered in her rural home.

programme.title: Killer Town
programme.desc: The small town of Enderby, British Columbia is shattered after well-loved residents, Derk Roelfsema and Audrey Foster-Roelfsema are found murdered in their own home.

programme.title: Killer Town
programme.desc: When a bomb kills a young paraplegic mother, authorities must determine if it was sent by a drug dealer seeking revenge or someone much closer to the victim

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Kimberly Greene-Medina
programme.desc: Kimberly Greene-Medina’s disappearance in 1996 remained unanswered until 2005, when her husband, Michael Medina, was arrested for killing his 16-month-old son, Degan, as an act of revenge against his second wife, Becky.

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Skylar Neese
programme.desc: On 6 July 2012, surveillance camera footage captured 16-year-old Skylar Neese sneaking out of her bedroom window and getting into a car at 12:30am. This was the last time Skylar was seen alive

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Dana Mackay
programme.desc: In July 2013, Dana Mackay was lying in bed when her husband’s mistress, along with two accomplices, broke into her home and beat her to death. Nicole Houchin, Nace Houchin and Gregory Crawford were part of a murder-for-hire scheme

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Ashley Mead
programme.desc: In Boulder, Colorado, Adam Densmore brutally murdered his ex-girlfriend, Ashley Mead, in their apartment and transported the body to his family’s home in Louisiana, where he proceeded to dismember it. He then drove to Oklahoma and scattered evidence.

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Raul Ortiz
programme.desc: In August 2014, Erin Robinson violently beat Raul Ortiz for allegedly making unwanted comments to his girlfriend. Robinson allegedly struck Ortiz three times in the face, knocking him out. Ortiz never regained consciousness.

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Sarah Ludemann
programme.desc: Caught in a dangerous love triangle, 19-year-old Rachel Wade stabbed 17-year-old Sarah Ludemann over Joshua Camacho. Camacho had begun a relationship with Ludemann, but kept in touch with his ex, Wade, sparking negative feelings between the two.

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Audreanna Zimmerman
programme.desc: In 2010 in Pensacola, Florida, 19-year-old Audreanna Zimmerman visited the mobile home of 16-year-old Britnee Miller in an attempt to 'patch things up' following a physical altercation between the two. Her visit turned out to be a deadly mistake.

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Heather Strong
programme.desc: In February 2009, Joshua Fulgham and Emilia Carr forced Heather Strong, Fulgham’s wife, to sign a document granting full custody of her children to Fulgham, then proceeded to suffocate her to death. The murder was thought to be motivated by jealousy.

programme.title: Green Eyed Killers: Christine Paolilla
programme.desc: Christine Paolilla: Aged just 17, Christine Paolilla and her drug-addicted boyfriend took the lives of four people including two of her friends in a horrific, jealousy-fuelled attack.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rose West
programme.desc: With her husband Fred, Rose West brutally murdered ten girls between 1973 and 1979, making her one of the most high profile serial killers in the UK.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Marchioness Disaster
programme.desc: The story of the Marchioness; a pleasure boat that collided with dredger Bowbelle and sank within minutes resulting in the loss of 51 lives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Clare Wood
programme.desc: In April 2007, Clare Wood met George Appleton on a dating website and they embarked on a relationship together. Unbeknownst to her, Appleton was a criminal with a severe history of violence against women.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Perfect Murder: Roger& David Cooper
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the case of two brothers who murdered a marketing manager on Christmas Eve 2014 using chloroform.
programme.sub-title: The Perfect Murder: Roger& David Cooper

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Signs of Abduction: From loss of time and bizarre hazy memories, to terrifying accounts of alien surgery, these are the hidden signs of alien abduction.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: For many people, the most credible UFO reports are those raised by law enforcement. We investigate the most significant reports from police records.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 10
programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Nazi UFOs: Could alien technology have helped the Nazis' rise to power? Experts question whether Hitler had extraterrestrial help in his quest for world domination.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 11
programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Between 1981 and 2011, cameras on NASA space shuttles have picked up many images of UFOs that experts still struggle to explain.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 9
programme.desc: We have always been told that mankind enjoys free will. Is this really true or have aliens created the means for controlling humanity?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Discover theories relating to why aliens are interested in visiting Earth, including the allures of the human mind and human civilisation.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: As the world continues to evolve into the digital age, people are capturing images in the sky that they are unable to explain.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: While many UFO sightings occur in rural locations with only a single eyewitness, there have been sightings over cities witnessed by hundreds.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 8
programme.desc: Take a fresh look at some classic alien encounters, UFOs and abductions, and discover several new stories.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: An in-depth look at the US government's classified and declassified investigations into UFO sightings.
programme.sub-title: NEW EPISODE The Freedom Of Information Act

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Explore the travel challenges that alien spaceships face, and discover whether humans can tackle interstellar flight.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: UFO Zones: Journey to the world's hottest UFO zones, where physical and paranormal phenomenons and unidentified objects and aliens are reported with alarming frequency.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: As humanity continues to search for alien life, experts give their analysis on how close we are to first contact.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Examine the possible components of aliens' protective second layer of skin.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: An in-depth look at the information obtained from people who have experienced alien abductions.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: With light from distant stars taking millions of years to reach us, how long might it take for alien life to answer any communication sent into space?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Antigravity: Experts discuss the possibility of advanced alien technology that could allow UFOs to fly at blinding speed or hover effortlessly a few feet above the ground.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: An investigation into a possible secret space force operated by America.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: In 1947, there was an unprecedented wave of UFO sightings that propelled the UFO phenomenon to the world stage. What were the US Air Force's findings?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: What are UFOs? Are they spacecraft? Or vessels of a different kind, from a place beyond imagination? Do they travel vast distances to reach planet Earth? And do they come in peace-- or to conquer?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Nearly three hundred million people worldwide claim to have been abducted by aliens. Many report being subjected to torturous medical experiments. Others return with unwelcome reminders of their ordeal. Are aliens creating human hybrids?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: In the 1960s we too our first steps into a vast new realm—bound for the Moon. But NASA astronauts would soon find they weren’t the only ones out there.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Some experts believe many atmospheric lightshows are actually caused by extraterrestrials. Do they pose an imminent threat to humanity?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Millions of people believe extraterrestrial live on Earth. Are the aliens here to enlighten us or destroy us? What if the worst-case scenario happened? What would aliens do? And how would they do it?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: They’re our first line of defense in the darkest of times. And they’re often the first responders to threats from another world. Over the past 70 years the world’s military forces have come face to face with thousands of unidentified objects.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Every day, millions take to the sky in commercial flights all over the world. But decades of evidence have revealed we may not be the world’s only frequent flyers. Are we in danger of losing control of our skies to beings from another world?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: It’s the stuff of nightmares. Emergency lines are flooded with calls as UFOs appear in the sky faster than authorities can track them.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Triangle Terror: For years, the flying saucer has been synonymous with UFO terror. But is it still the spacecraft that frightens us the most? Or has fear taken a new shape?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Alien Achilles Heel. Many believe aliens are already here on Earth. Could they be plotting an attack? And if so, can humans survive their weapons' terrifying capabilities?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Countdown To Disclosure: Evidence suggests the world's governments could be on the verge of announcing everything they know about UFOs. What will this mean for our society?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 7
programme.desc: Experts open previously off-limits files in a bid to answer some of the most mysterious questions surrounding UFOs and extra-terrestrial life.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Aliens on the Moon
programme.desc: A look at the possibility that life began on Mars and travelled to Earth. Plus, if aliens exist, what plans do they have in store for us? Experts investigate.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: UFO Portal L.A.
programme.desc: Experts investigate why so many UFO sightings take place in Los Angeles, determining that the answer may lie underwate

programme.title: Moesha: Rhythm Dues
programme.desc: Mo encourages CPQ to submit their latest song to the local radio station for their “play it or stuff it” segment. When the song is played, it is well received. Q has written lyrics over a Morris Day song, and Morris shows up at “The Hole” where he plays.

programme.title: Moesha: The Double Date
programme.desc: Mo has been dating Bridgewood classmate Jeremy since her break up with Q.

programme.title: Moesha: He Ain’t Heavy – He’s Dee’s Brother
programme.desc: Mo gets a crush on Dee’s very attractive younger brother when he comes to visit.
programme.sub-title: He Ain’t Heavy – He’s Dee’s Brother

programme.title: Moesha: It’s My Paper And I’ll Cry If I Want To
programme.desc: Mo decides to publish her own Bridgewood newspaper when editor Mary Ellen refuses to add her to the staff of the existing paper.
programme.sub-title: It’s My Paper And I’ll Cry If I Want To

programme.title: Moesha: The “Short” Story
programme.desc: Mo’s group at the Den includes a short kid known as “Biggie”.

programme.title: Moesha: She’s Back
programme.desc: A famous female broadcast journalist who used to be friends with Frank speaks at Bridgewood, so Mo invites her to their house for dinner.

programme.title: Moesha: This Time You’ve Gone Too Far
programme.desc: Mo, Kim, Hakeem, Niecy, Morgan, Marco, and Chris head off on a road trip to San Diego.
programme.sub-title: This Time You’ve Gone Too Far

programme.title: Moesha: Body Language
programme.desc: Mo, Kim and Niecy play a game of “Truth or Dare”.

programme.title: Moesha: Model Employee
programme.desc: Rather than asking Frank every time she wants something, Mo decides to get a part-time job at the Class Act where Hakeem works.

programme.title: Moesha: Pajama Jam
programme.desc: Mo asks Frank if she can go to a slumber party at Morgan’s house.

programme.title: Moesha: Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money
programme.desc: Moesha wants to get cable and her own phone now that she has a job and is making money.
programme.sub-title: Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money

programme.title: Moesha: A House Is Not A Home
programme.desc: Moesha is living with Andell after having moved out of Frank & Dee’s home.
programme.sub-title: A House Is Not A Home

programme.title: Moesha: Moesha Meets Brandy
programme.desc: Moesha meets Brandy at a concert and we discover that Moesha is having a romance with Aaron, a college student.

programme.title: Moesha: Homecoming
programme.desc: With a little help from Myles and a visit from Grandma Mitchell, Moesha moves back home and returns to Crenshaw High.

programme.title: Moesha: Hello! What’s This?
programme.desc: Dee finds a joint in the couch while cleaning the house. Frank and Dee assume and accuse Mo, but Moesha is shocked when she catches Myles smoking.

programme.title: Moesha: Psyche Your Mind
programme.desc: Based on the family turmoil, the family begins counseling, but Moesha doesn’t want to air the family’s 'dirty laundry'.

programme.title: Moesha: Teacher
programme.desc: Moesha is attracted to a substitute teacher at Crenshaw. After they kiss, he leaves the school for 'personal reasons'.

programme.title: Moesha: I Know What You Did In The Third Grade
programme.desc: Moesha and her friends are stalked by a student who was just released from jail who they haven’t seen since the third grade when he got into trouble because of them. Parody of Brandy’s feature 'I Still Know What You Did Last Summer…'
programme.sub-title: I Know What You Did In The Third Grade

programme.title: Moesha: A Terrible Thing Happened On My Tour Of College
programme.desc: Moesha and her friends are invited to a college to attend a Prospective Freshman Weekend and someone slips a 'roofie' in her drink. Her boyfriend Aaron keeps her from being taken advantage of.
programme.sub-title: A Terrible Thing Happened On My Tour Of College

programme.title: Moesha: Birth Control
programme.desc: Moesha gets birth control pills when she decides she would like to lose her virginity to Aaron. However, things get complicated when Dee finds the pills in her purse.

programme.title: Moesha: A Class Act Christmas
programme.desc: Moesha, Hakeem, and the other employees of the Class Act are held hostage by a man dressed as Santa Claus protesting age discrimination.

programme.title: Moesha: The Crush
programme.desc: A friend of Myles who has a crush on Moesha pretends he cannot read so she can tutor him. However, after an article she writes about the success she has had with the little student is published in the LA Times, Moesha learns a hard lesson.

programme.title: Moesha: Barking Up The Wrong Tree
programme.desc: Hakeem accepts a job as assistant manager of the Class Act claiming there are financial difficulties at home. Moesha, concerned about his college education, confronts his mother who denies his claims.

programme.title: Moesha: Life Imitating Art
programme.desc: Racial tensions mount in Moesha’s high school after a mural depicting Latin American hero Caesar Chavez is purposely defaced.

programme.title: Moesha: OhMiGod, Fanatic
programme.desc: After Moesha and Kim have a huge fight, Moesha is surprised when MTV shows up at her door to film her bringing her best friend Kim to meet her favorite singer. Leanne Rimes guest stars.

programme.title: Moesha: The Rite Stuff
programme.desc: Moesha wants to boycott her debutante ball when she learns one of the girls is thrown out of the ceremony because she is pregnant.

programme.title: Moesha: I Love Moesha
programme.desc: When Moesha is once again rejected from her friends’ band, we enter a black and white parody of I LOVE LUCY wherein Lucy (Moesha) tries to get into Ricky’s show.

programme.title: Moesha: Home Is Where The Car Is
programme.desc: Moesha is disappointed when she is not appointed valedictorian of her senior class. She discovers the boy who has been appointed is homeless.
programme.sub-title: Home Is Where The Car Is

programme.title: Moesha: Girl’s Night In
programme.desc: At a slumber party, Moesha and her friends reminisce (with clips from past episodes) about the past four years together.

programme.title: Moesha: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Kim’s mother, Nikki, is re-entering a journey she started 18 years ago. With Dee’s help, she will be completing her education to receive a high school diploma.

programme.title: Moesha: Had To Be You
programme.desc: An old boyfriend of Andell’s comes back into her life and proposes to her. Moesha tries to keep Andell and Moesha’s Uncle Bernie together before it’s too late.

programme.title: Moesha: The Prom
programme.desc: Moesha’s date for the proms cancels at the last minute. She goes unescorted and meets a handsome mysterious boy who disappears at midnight. She tries to find him, but to no avail.

programme.title: Moesha: A Concerted Effort (Part 2)
programme.desc: Mo cries the lovesick blues on a phone-in radio show. The DJ, feeling sorry for Mo, gives her four free tickets to an upcoming concert.

programme.title: Moesha: The Regulations Of Love
programme.desc: Kim unintentionally lands a boyfriend while trying to make Hakeem jealous.

programme.title: Moesha: There’s No Place Like The Mitchell Home
programme.desc: Moesha runs the Den into the ground while Andell recuperates at the Mitchell home from knee surgery.
programme.sub-title: There’s No Place Like The Mitchell Home

programme.title: Moesha: Talk Of The Town
programme.desc: Mo organizes a Town Hall meeting when local merchants and neighbors threaten to shut down the Bomb Bay because of the young noisy crowds.

programme.title: Moesha: Back To Africa
programme.desc: Moesha may have to bow out of the class trip to Africa when Frank invests all of the Mitchell savings in a Saturn dealership.

programme.title: Moesha: Guess Q’s Coming To Dinner
programme.desc: Mo is shirking her duties at home and her grades are falling. Frank blames Q.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Sheffield, Windsor Revisits
programme.desc: Adrenaline junkies Deborah and Justin Beeley had more stuff than they could cope with while Matt Rushworth's entire life could be squeezed into his well travelled backpack. Could Phil and Kirstie find fitting homes for both of them?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Derbyshire
programme.desc: This week Phil and Kirstie are searching in Derbyshire, where their first couple Bryone & Daniel Burton have moved from London, along with daughter Ruby.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: South Hertfordshire
programme.desc: Martine Wright and Nick Wiltshire met in 2003 and are getting married in just two months' time.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit: Sheffield
programme.desc: A year after two troublesome searches, Phil heads back to Sheffield to see how a couple of doctors and a firefighter are faring.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: South Bucks
programme.desc: With both sets of house hunters determined to have it all, Kirstie and Phil are going to have to use all the tricks of the trade if they're going to help them move forward and hopefully into the house of their dreams!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Yorkshire
programme.desc: This week, we're back in Yorkshire catching up with lotto millionaires Roger and Lara Griffiths. Just over a year ago, this lucky pair scooped almost two million quid on the lottery and were desperate to splash out on a swanky new pad.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Bristol & Avon
programme.desc: This week they're in the South West of the country taking on two sets of hapless house hunters.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Brighton, Sussex
programme.desc: A first-time buyer after a perfect pad near the Brighton hot spots and a couple who know exactly what they like-and exactly what they don't...

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Bath
programme.desc: This week we're back catching up with Sadie Yukon and Simon Gwillian, a picky pair who really put us through the mill.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Norwich
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are called to the rescue of faint-hearted first time buyers, Helen and Paul. Scared off by serious-sounding surveys this hapless pair have loved and lost their dream home no less than three times in the last twelve months.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Jersey
programme.desc: This week we're on Jersey hunting for a fabulous family home for the young couple Nick and Katrina. We show them the many highs and lows this Channel Island has to offer.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit: Hastings
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil revisit two couples in Hastings, Jakki and Scott, and newlyweds Nicola and Chris, only to find that one couple is now in a very different place.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit 2: Jersey/Warwickshire
programme.desc: This week we're going back to visit two very different sets of house hunters who had one thing on their minds – space, and they wanted lots of it!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Wiltshire
programme.desc: One couple want room for 4 children and a 4x4 business -whilst the other want a place that will 'give them a big hug' whenever they walk through the door!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Midlothian
programme.desc: Professional rugby player Matt and girlfriend Amy have been giving the property market a try for over a year but so far, have failed to score.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Yorkshire & Guildford
programme.desc: After winning one point eight million on the lottery, they needed a new house.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Brighton
programme.desc: Armed with the house-hunters' requirements, laptops and a surgical link to their mobile phones, Kirstie and Phil blitz the market looking for that perfect new place.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: London Northside
programme.desc: Kirstie's house hunter is London born and bred and has viewed a whopping 90 properties already-whilst Phil's couple have yet to view a single place and have no idea where to buy.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Wicklow
programme.desc: This week we're helping a young mother achieve her dream of moving back to Ireland. Sue Wessell longs to return to the place where she grew up and has persuaded husband Chris and their two children to up sticks and relocate to the Emerald Isle.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Devon Revisits
programme.desc: Newlyweds Sam and Jamie needed Kirstie's help to start afresh in a new home. Whilst Geoff and Sharon were making the move to Torquay and needed to find a home big enough for them, their kids, and Sharon's mum!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Bristol
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil come to the aid of newly qualified doctor Ian Ensum, as he takes his first steps on the property ladder.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Oxfordshire
programme.desc: Our first set of house-hunters are the closest thing to all action heroes Phil and Kirstie have ever had.

programme.title: Dynasty: Blake Goes to Jail
programme.desc: Blake must stand trial for the murder of Steven's lover; Krystle returns home to be with Blake. Fallon testified in her father's behalf but Steven refutes her statements in court.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Testimony
programme.desc: On the witness stand, Blake maintains his innocence; Matthew learns of Claudia's affair with Steven; Claudia and daughter Lindsay are involved in an automobile accident; The prosecution calls in a mysterious witness that Blake is surprised to see.

programme.title: Dynasty: Enter Alexis
programme.desc: Blake's first wife Alexis testifies that Blake is capable of violence; Matthew takes off with Lindsay; Alexis tells Steven of her love for him; the jury announces they have reached a verdict.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Verdict
programme.desc: Blake is found guilty; Krystle reunites with her husband; Blake strikes Steven; a pregnant Krystle suffers a fall; Alexis moves onto the Carrington estate.

programme.title: Dynasty: Alexis' Secret
programme.desc: Krystle orders Alexis from the mansion; Blake instructs his attorney to cut Steven out of his will; Claudia attempts suicide; Alexis tells Steven that he is Blake's only child.

programme.title: Dynasty: Fallon's Father
programme.desc: Alexis tells Cecil Colby that Fallon is his daughter; Blake and Steven are urged to reconcile; Dr. Toscanni pulls an unconscious Steven from the pool and rushes him to the hospital.

programme.title: Dynasty: Reconciliation
programme.desc: Steven returns to the mansion to recover from injuries sustained by his fall; Krystle's niece, Sammy Jo, arrives to spend a few weeks with the Carrigtons; Alexis hires a private investigator to delve into Krystle's past.

programme.title: Dynasty: Viva Las Vegas
programme.desc: Nick reveals to his sister that Blake is responsible for the death of their younger brother; Sammy Jo and Steven spend the night together; Alexis shoots at Krystle's horse, causing her to be thrown to the ground and hospitalized.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Miscarriage
programme.desc: Krystle loses her baby; Steven and Sammy Jo begin dating, although Steven is in love with Claudia. Fallon continues her affair with Nick, and is jealous to find him visiting Krystle in the hospital.

programme.title: Dynasty: Mid-East Wedding
programme.desc: Fallon learns she is pregnant and tells Jeff she is having an abortion; he agrees to divorce her but only if she bears the child. Krystle remains despondent over the loss of her baby; Claudia takes a job with Denver-Carrington.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Alexis tricks Blake into coming to Rome and further traps him by hiring a gossip photographer to snap suggestive photos of the two; Jeff begs Fallon to cancel her abortion; as Nick confronts Krystle, his consolation gives way to passion.

programme.title: Dynasty: Sammy Jo and Steven Marry
programme.desc: Nick tells Krystle of his deep feelings for her; Steven and Sammy Jo marry; Fallon cancels her abortion; after the scandal caused by the papparazzi photos of Alexis and Blake, Krystle leaves her husband.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Car Explosion
programme.desc: Nick persuades Krystle to leave Blake; Steven and Sammy Jo announce they are married; Blake is attacked by gangsters and blinded when his car is bombed.

programme.title: Dynasty: Blake's Blindness
programme.desc: Blake remains blinded following the explosion; Nick asks Krystle to divorce her husband and marry him; Blake investigates Nick's past; Alexis is determined to drive Krystle from Denver.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Hearing
programme.desc: Fallon confesses her love to Nick, but he is interested in Krystle; Blake's attempts to bring gangster Logan Rhinewood before a Crime Committee hearing are thwarted; Sammy Jo tells Alexis she knows her secret; Blake receives an anonymous letter.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Iago Syndrome
programme.desc: Krystle says goodbye to Nick and leaves Denver; Blake offers to adopt Jeff; Cecil blackmails Claudia; Nick accepts Blake's explanation of his brother's death.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Party
programme.desc: At a party in her honor, Sammy Jo embarrasses the Carringtons and reveals to Fallon that Blake is not her father; Jeff leaves the party with Claudia; Blake tells Krystle his eyesight is restored; Fallon is left unconscious after a car accident.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Baby
programme.desc: Fallon gives birth to a son; Alexis offers Sammy Jo $25,000 to leave Denver; Jeff and Claudia make love; Krystle accuses Alexis of killing her baby; Alexis learns Krystle and Blake are not legally married.
