programme.sub-title: The Model Marriage / Too Clothes For Comfort / Original Sin / This Year's Model / Vogue Rogue - Part 1

programme.title: The Love Boat: Lost And Found / Then There Were Two / Maid For Each Other
programme.desc: Gopher's wealthy aunt (Jane Powell) meets a wealthy man (Howard Keel) and is afraid someone may discover her heartbreaking secret.
programme.sub-title: Lost And Found / Then There Were Two / Maid For Each Other

programme.title: The Love Boat: Tony And Julie / Separate Beds / America's Sweetheart
programme.desc: Julie falls hopelessly in love with a handsome doctor (Anthony Andrews).
programme.sub-title: Tony And Julie / Separate Beds / America's Sweetheart

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Expedition Part 2 / The Mongala/ Julie's Replacement/ The Three R's/ Professor's Wife - Part 2
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing walks Julie down the aisle for her marriage to Tony, the handsome Australian doctor who proposed to her at the end of Season 4.
programme.sub-title: The Expedition Part 2 / The Mongala/ Julie's Replacement/ The Three R's/ Professor's Wife - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: Return Of The Captains Lady / Love Ain't Illegal/ The Irresistible Man
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing considers getting remarried while an unscrupulous businessman leaves his girlfriend on the ship with his shy partner. Plus, a man wreaks havoc while trying to attract the attention of a woman, much to the chagrin of another female passenger.
programme.sub-title: Return Of The Captains Lady / Love Ain't Illegal/ The Irresistible Man

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Lady From Laramie / Vicki Swings/ Phantom Bride
programme.desc: A provincial woman finds herself hopelessly in love with a paid escort, while Gopher sees the ghost of a man and wife still occupying a room aboard the ship.
programme.sub-title: The Lady From Laramie / Vicki Swings/ Phantom Bride

programme.title: The Love Boat: Chef's Special / Kleinschmidt/ Beginning Anew
programme.desc: A beautiful, fun-loving woman with a secret (Joan Fontaine) reunites with a former love (Richard Basehart) aboard the ship. Plus, an inept German detective almost turns the Pacific Princess upside down when his employer's jewels are stolen.

programme.title: The Love Boat: Love Honor And Obey / Gladys And Agnes/ Radioactive Isaac
programme.desc: As widow tries to set up her spinster sister with an English lord, Isaac's overtures to a beautiful girl fall apart when his new tooth filling starts receiving strange transmissions.n may lead to divorce.
programme.sub-title: Love Honor And Obey / Gladys And Agnes/ Radioactive Isaac

programme.title: The Love Boat: Isaac The Marriage Counselor / The Incredible Hunk/Jewels And Jim
programme.desc: The ship shudders when an entertainer hired by Julie turns out to be an exotic dancer and he doesn't want his secret revealed. Meanwhile, Isaac learns that a former passenger misunderstood his advice and divorced his wife.
programme.sub-title: Isaac The Marriage Counselor / The Incredible Hunk/Jewels And Jim

programme.title: The Love Boat: Take A Letter Vicky / The Floating Bridge Game/ The Joy Of Celibacy
programme.desc: An emotionally cold woman finds herself falling for a Romeo with hot ideas. Meanwhile, a widow's romance with an attractive dentist is hindered by her nosy, card-playing companions (Rose Marie, Jan Clayton, and Lucille Benson).
programme.sub-title: Take A Letter Vicky / The Floating Bridge Game/ The Joy Of Celibacy

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Viking's Son / Love Boat - Pride Of The Pacific/ Separate Vacations/ The Experiment - Part 2
programme.desc: An emotionally cold woman finds herself falling for a Romeo with hot ideas. Meanwhile, a widow's romance with an attractive dentist is hindered by her nosy, card-playing companions (Rose Marie, Jan Clayton, and Lucille Benson).
programme.sub-title: The Viking's Son / Love Boat - Pride Of The Pacific/ Separate Vacations/ The Experiment - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: Good Neighbors / Captain's Portrait/ Familiar Faces
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing develops an unusual relationship with a lovely woman who is tasked with painting his portrait.
programme.sub-title: Good Neighbors / Captain's Portrait/ Familiar Faces

programme.title: The Love Boat: I Don't Play Anymore / Gopher's Roommate/ Crazy For You
programme.desc: Gopher is shocked when he finds his old college roommate is different than he remembers. At the same time, an embittered and retired pianist agrees to perform again in order to pay for a handicapped woman's surgery.
programme.sub-title: I Don't Play Anymore / Gopher's Roommate/ Crazy For You

programme.title: The Love Boat: Green, But Not Jolly / Past Perfect Love/ Instant Family
programme.desc: A man falls in love with a beautiful woman who believes the couple was married in a past life 100 years earlier. The crew's amusement at Julie's new hairstyle creates pandemonium.
programme.sub-title: Green, But Not Jolly / Past Perfect Love/ Instant Family

programme.title: The Love Boat: Isaac Gets Physical / She Brought Her Mother Along/ Cold Feet
programme.desc: A female doctor's temperature rises when she meets Isaac, who tries to avoid his physical exam so he can hide a congenital condition. Meanwhile, a young woman and her mother learn devastating truths about each other when they meet the daughter's fiance.
programme.sub-title: Isaac Gets Physical / She Brought Her Mother Along/ Cold Feet

programme.title: The Love Boat: Burl Of My Dreams / Meet The Author/ Rhymes, Riddles And Romance
programme.desc: While the captain's daughter falls for Gopher, a book publisher flips a page in the book of love when trying to sign a burly author who happens to be accompanied by his attractive niece. Plus, an unusual couple looks for treasure.
programme.sub-title: Burl Of My Dreams / Meet The Author/ Rhymes, Riddles And Romance

programme.title: The Love Boat: His Girl Friday / A Wife For Wilfred/ The Girl Who Stood Still
programme.desc: Crew members become matchmakers when a man offers a handsome reward to anyone who can find him a good woman. Plus, a teen girl traveling with her mother faces potential humiliation when she runs into a former boyfriend.
programme.sub-title: His Girl Friday / A Wife For Wilfred/ The Girl Who Stood Still

programme.title: Melrose Place: Moving Violations
programme.desc: Jane and Sydney escape when Jane hits Richard in the gut with a shovel. As a deputy is driving Jane and Syd to meet Samantha, they see Richard come out of a grocery store. Richard is killed in a shootout.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Hunka, Hunka Burnin Love
programme.desc: Peter fires Sydney after learning that she sold his house (which he had listed as a company asset) to pay the practice's debts. Peter has to move into Amanda's apartment.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Un-Janed Melody
programme.desc: Jane saves Jake and Alison from the fire. Jane apologizes for her outburst but later tries to seduce Billy to make the couple jealous.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Jane's Addiction
programme.desc: Jane slashes Alison's tires. Jake catches Jane following Alison and him and orders her to stop stalking them. Jane files a restraining order against Jake.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Young Doctors in Heat
programme.desc: Amanda and Peter continue to fight constantly. Peter decides to stay with Michael and Kimberly for a while.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Mission Interpersonal
programme.desc: Taylor reveals that she is the younger sister of Peter's late wife Beth. She hangs out with Peter while Amanda is away on a business trip, and helps him pick out a ring for her.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Farewell Mike's Concubine
programme.desc: Kimberly orders Megan to stop "servicing" Michael, but he refuses to stay away from her. Michael cuts off Megan's relationship with her clients.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Nice Work If You Can Get It
programme.desc: Craig takes the reins of D&D. Arthur pays Amanda to keep an eye on Craig so that the business can stay afloat until he resumes control.
programme.sub-title: Nice Work If You Can Get It

programme.title: Melrose Place: Sole Sister
programme.desc: Kyle rejects Sydney and vows to make his marriage work. Jane also pushes Syd away as she searches for her birth mother. She learns that her mother is a former actress named Sherry (Larson) Doucette.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Quest for Mother
programme.desc: Kimberly rejects Michael's final attempt at a reconciliation. He decides to stay with Matt for a while, but Kimberly urges Megan to get Michael back. Dan pressures Matt to move in with him.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Crazy Love
programme.desc: Sydney resents Jane's new relationship with her mother. Sherry lies about the identity of Jane's biological father but later reveals that he was a producer with whom she had slept with to win a part.

programme.title: Melrose Place: The Accidental Doctor
programme.desc: Michael tries to reunite Peter and Amanda in the hopes that Peter will again make a contribution at the office. Kyle tries to play matchmaker at his birthday party, but Amanda isn't ready to talk to Peter.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Escape from L. A.
programme.desc: Michael signs the divorce papers and marries Megan during their trip to Las Vegas. Peter learns that Kimberly has a brain tumor, and breaks the news to Michael. Jane is robbed at gunpoint at the boutique.

programme.title: Melrose Place: The Eyes of the Storm
programme.desc: Michael insists on spending time with Kimberly after learning of her terminal illness. She reveals to Megan that she will crash her car during the next rainstorm.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Better Homes and Condos
programme.desc: Kimberly pulls Michael from the wrecked vehicle while Megan calls for help. Michael experiences internal bleeding and flatlines.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Catch Her in a Lie
programme.desc: Megan is furious to learn that Michael has slept with Kimberly. She forgives Michael, but then stakes out the beach house and nearly runs over Kimberly.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Men are From Melrose
programme.desc: Nick follows Taylor to Peter's house and witnesses a romantic encounter. He later photographs the couple's late-night rendezvous at a golf course. Megan discovers that Kimberly's illness is in remission.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Frames R Us
programme.desc: Nick drops hints about the photos to Peter. Taylor drugs Nick while Peter removes the pictures from his apartment. When Kyle arrives home, Taylor pretends that Nick is attacking her.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Screams From A Marriage
programme.desc: Amanda rejects Craig, who seeks a relationship after their night together. Kyle realizes that Taylor has been lying to him.

programme.title: Melrose Place: 101 Damnations
programme.desc: Michael takes Megan back after learning that she has returned to the streets. A woman sues Sydney after she is injured by a display at the boutique.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Meeting Dad’s Girlfriend
programme.desc: Love definitely isn’t in the air when Sabrina meets her dad’s new girlfriend.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: As Westbridge Turns
programme.desc: Sinister plots, tumultuous romance, danger, and intrigue – just another day at Westbridge High, after Sabrina casts a spell wishing for more excitement.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Great Mistake
programme.desc: Sabrina finds herself in a little trouble when she sneaks out of the house after being grounded.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Crucible
programme.desc: Sabrina is put on trial for witchcraft when her class visits historical Salem, Massachusetts.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Troll Bride
programme.desc: When Sabrina is tricked into marrying a troll, only a prince – a.k.a. Harvey – can save her.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Gets Her License (Part 1)
programme.desc: Sabrina's 17th birthday turns stressful when she has to start studying for her witch's license.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Gets Her License (Part 2)
programme.desc: Sabrina learns about the responsibilities that come with using magic when she earns her witch’s learner’s permit.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dummy For Love
programme.desc: Sabrina takes advantage of Vice Principal Kraft’s attraction to Aunt Hilda.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dante’s Inferno
programme.desc: Sabrina learns not to date outside of her realm after a bad evening with a witch boy.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: A Doll’s Story
programme.desc: Babysitting becomes a test of ingenuity when Sabrina’s bratty little cousin turns her into a doll.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina The Boy
programme.desc: The mysteries of men are revealed when Sabrina poses as a boy to figure out how they think.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: A River Of Candy Corn Runs Through It
programme.desc: Halloween is a scary night indeed when Sabrina and her aunts try to throw a normal party
programme.sub-title: A River Of Candy Corn Runs Through It

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Inna Gadda Sabrina
programme.desc: A time ball sends the town of Westbridge grooving back to the 1960s.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Witch Trash
programme.desc: A family feud erupts when hillbilly cousins (Loni Anderson and Alice Ghostley) arrive from the Other Realm.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: To Tell A Mortal
programme.desc: It’s Friday the 13th, and for one day only, Sabrina can tell her friends she’s a witch.

programme.desc: Sabrina’s lack of attention with detail causes bad side effects to her magic.
programme.sub-title: Oh What A Tangled Spell She Weaves

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Claus
programme.desc: A special visitor from the Other Realm helps Sabrina recapture the true spirit of Christmas

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Little Big Kraft
programme.desc: Tired of Mr. Kraft’s harsh rules, Sabrina concocts a potion to make him act like a teenager.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: 5 Easy Pieces Of Libby
programme.desc: Libby is even harder to figure out after Sabrina accidentally turns her into a jigsaw puzzle.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Finger Lickin’ Flu
programme.desc: Sabrina catches the flu in her pointing finger and accidentally transfers her magic to a teacher.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina & The Beanstalk
programme.desc: Sabrina’s beanstalk is right out of a fairytale, but it’s more like a nightmare for Harvey

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Equalizer
programme.desc: Roland the troll takes Valentine’s Day too far when he steals Sabrina’s heart.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Band Show
programme.desc: Egos soar out of control when Sabrina uses magic to become a rock star.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: My Nightmare The Car
programme.desc: A talking car causes Sabrina a big headache.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Quiz Show
programme.desc: Sabrina realizes the importance of her Quizmaster after she inadvertently gets him fired.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Fear Strikes Up A Conversation

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Rumor Mill
programme.desc: Gossip flies when Sabrina does witch community service in the Other Realm rumor mill.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: When Teens Collide
programme.desc: When Sabrina switches personalities with Libby, she uses her magic and newly found popularity.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Mom vs. Magic (Season Finale)
programme.desc: Sabrina faces the most difficult decision of her life when she must choose between her mom and her magic

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Disney World

programme.desc: After her aunts decide to go their separate ways, Sabrina has to choose which of the two should raise her.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Boy Was My Face Red
programme.desc: An embarrassing moment leaves Sabrina red in the face.

programme.desc: Sabrina learns she has to solve the family secret before she can use her Witches License.
programme.sub-title: It’s a Mad Mad Mad Mad (Season Premiere)

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Pancake Madness
programme.desc: An addiction to pancakes threatens to send Sabrina’s life spinning out of control.

programme.desc: Halloween turns truly frightful when a demented doll takes charge of the festivities.

programme.desc: Laa-Laa fetches the others to watch a little girl called Catherine play with her toy farm. A magical new event happens in Teletubbyland – Bo Peep has lost her sheep and doesn’t know where to find them.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children visit and aquarium to learn how things swim in the water. The Teletubbies play an in and out game when a cloud appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies walk across Teletubbyland to watch some children paint some pictures in a very interesting and messy way. It’s time for Tubby Custard, but Po makes too much Tubby Custard and the custard goes all over Home Hill. Noo Noo tidies up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a figure of 8 round the hills and then watch as some children visit a pier. The children look at the sea and watch some men fishing from the end of the pier.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and makes the Teletubbies laugh. They watch some children exploring the colour green. The Teletubbies love green.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and the Teletubbies dance, before watching two girls do some Flamenco dancing with their teacher. It's time for the Teletubbies to wash their feet. They love using their Tubby Sponges.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po makes some adjustments on the switch panel and then joins the others to watch Andy Brown and two children looking in a pond for frogs. Laa-Laa is looking at the flowers, but Dipsy keeps getting in the way.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po loves jumping. She jumps inside Home Hill, then jumps off to watch some children go blackberry picking. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po play a hiding game. Laa-Laa can't see them so she decides to play a game with her ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky is looking for the other Teletubbies. He can’t see any of them so he shouts out ‘Big Hug’ and all the Teletubbies come running for a big hug.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love Tubby Toast, and Teletubbies love children. So the Teletubbies stop to watch Ned working on his bicycle. Po finds a flag and leads Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa on a march around Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun sliding down into Home Hill. They watch some children painting circus pictures before the Lion and Bear appear in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A little boy goes for a ride on his Grandad’s motorbike and sidecar. In Teletubbyland, Po went to see Tinky Winky on her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po feels like having some Tubby Custard. The Teletubbies watch some children visiting a conservatory and drawing cacti. Tinky Winky goes for a walk. Five birds appear in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies dance round the flowers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy & Laa-Laa hug and dance and then join the others to watch some children and Andy Brown play catching leaves in Autumn. T

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies jump out to say "Eh-oh" before watching a girl paint a picture of her dad. A voice trumpet rises and sings the nursery rhyme Looby Loo, the Tubbies join in and dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to say "Meow". The Tubbies watch three boys play with their new kittens. A voice trumpet rises and announces that it is 'Time to get up.' But the Teletubbies are too tired and go back to bed.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children looking through a telescope at the moon. In Teletubbyland, Po is going round and round.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It’s time for Tubby Toast. Dipsy and Laa-Laa are so excited, they bounce up and down on their seats so much they fall off. They sit up to watch some children watching some birds in a bird bath.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po counts to three. The Teletubbies watch Sam have a ride in a helicopter. Laa-Laa's ball gets stuck up a tree and three magic ships sail through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies join in with the game when a voice trumpet sings the Grand Old Duke of York. The magic windmill brings a musical transmission featuring King Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children visit a Sealife centre to look at seahorses. The Teletubbies go looking for interesting things. Tinky Winky sees his reflection in a puddle. Dipsy sees different clouds.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance around the trees and watch some children drum with their friend Norris. Tinky Winky dance on the roof of Home Hill and wakes up the other Tubbies.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some girls live with their Mum and Dad on a boat. They show us inside the boat. The Teletubbies come out of the house. Tinky Winky and Po go left and Dipsy and Laa-Laa go right.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney brutally murdered two of his girlfriends, dismembered their bodies and dumped them in canals. In a macabre twist, he hid clues in his abstract paintings, and five other girlfriends of his are currently still missing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The parents of innocent 11-year-old Rhys Jones, shot on his way home from a football club in Liverpool 2007, speak about the harrowing day when they lost their son.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Shipman
programme.desc: Doctor Harold Shipman was convicted of killing 15 patients in 2000, but he is thought to have killed up to 215. Discover how he was eventually caught by detectives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Raoul Moat
programme.desc: The hunt for Raoul Moat was one of the largest in British history, after he wounded his ex-girlfriend, killed her new partner and shot and blinded police constable David Rathband.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993, gay men in London were being terrorised by a brutal killer. Colin Ireland, a loner from Southend, had made a macabre resolution – to become a serial killer.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jimmy Savile
programme.desc: Jimmy Savile: From national treasure to one of the UK's most notorious sex offenders: this is the story of how the revelation of Jimmy Savile's crimes left the UK reeling.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Lee Rigby
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Philpotts
programme.desc: When six children died in a house fire in Allenton, Derby in May 2012 it shocked the nation. Parents Mick and Mairead Philpott were charged with the murders. We uncover the reasons behind the arson attack on their own home.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Tobin
programme.desc: Peter Tobin is one of the most notorious serial killers in the UK. From 1991–2006 he raped and murdered three young girls; Dinah McNicol, Vicky Hamilton and Angelika Kluk. Yet even prior to these horrific murders he had a history of violence.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Gemma McCluskie
programme.desc: The harrowing story of EastEnders actress Gemma McCluskie, who was murdered by her older brother in the flat they shared in east London in 2012.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Wrongly Released
programme.desc: This special episode shines a light on criminals who have been brought to justice for one crime only to be released to commit further terrible acts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Dunblane Massacre
programme.desc: In an emotional tribute, witnesses and victims' relatives look back on the tragic murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in Scotland, 15 years ago.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The London Nail Bombings
programme.desc: London was brought to a standstill in April 1999 when 23-year-old political extremist David Copeland caused terror with a bombing campaign.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Keith Blakelock
programme.desc: In 1985, PC Keith Blakelock was murdered during a riot on a housing estate in London, triggered after a black woman died of heart failure during a house search. Nobody has been brought to justice for his murder, despite three investigations.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Jill Dando
programme.desc: Documentary examining the unsolved murder of British TV presenter and newsreader Jill Dando, who was shot outside her house in Fulham, London, in 1999.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Importance of Being Frank
programme.desc: Joan starts dating her client's very attractive accountant Keith, only to be stalked by his estranged wife.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Everything Fishy Ain’t Fish
programme.desc: Joan learns that Maya's husband may be cheating on her and must face whether or not it's a good idea to tell her friend. Toni dates a City Councilman who can help further her career.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Never A Bridesmaid
programme.desc: Maya and her partner Darnell decide they want to renew their vows and have a formal wedding ceremony. Joan offers to help with the event and to have it at her home.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Remains of the Date
programme.desc: Joan is determined to play the ultimate Halloween prank on William and succeeds. Meanwhile, Toni is left to deal with her dates strange sexual fetish for stuffed animals.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Fried Turkey
programme.desc: On a holiday that emphasizes togetherness, Joan struggles with feelings of loneliness and separation on Thanksgiving.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Pregnant Pause
programme.desc: Baby has never been in Joan's vocabulary until she gets the scare of her life and a new perspective on her priorities. As a result, the girls come together to give their love and support, showing their true Girlfriend friendship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The List
programme.desc: Dreading another dateless weekend, the girls and William try Maya's suggestion to make a list of what they want in a mate.

programme.title: Girlfriends: They’ve Gotta Have It
programme.desc: Joan's new boyfriend Sean has not made a move on her after three weeks of dating and she is beginning to wonder why.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Bad Timing
programme.desc: Joan begins to question her relationship with Sean when he gives her a power drill for Valentine's and doesn't hear from him for three days.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Old Dog
programme.desc: Joan decides it is time to give in on her three-month rule and she is finally going to have sex with her boyfriend Sean.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Declaration of Lynndependence
programme.desc: Joan and Toni decide that Lynn needs to learn to be independent. They give her a week to find a job, then she is on her own.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Friends, Colleagues, Brothers
programme.desc: Joan discovers that being the only black woman at her firm has many disadvantages and she is constantly ignored and overlooked.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Diss-regard
programme.desc: Maya checks into the hospital for her fibroid surgery and Joan has trouble managing her time between her boyfriend and her friends.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Burning Vagina Monologues
programme.desc: Joan is stressing because Sean hasn't told her that he loves her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Kiss Before Lying
programme.desc: Sean notices that Joan always flirts with Davis. He gets jealous and tells Joan that she can never see Davis again or go back to 847.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Loose Lips Sink Relationships
programme.desc: Joan opens her mouth and tells Greg about Toni and Clay.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Jamaic-Up?
programme.desc: Joan takes the girls to the Sinbad Music Festival to help ease Toni to get over her breakup with Greg.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Fallout
programme.desc: The ladies return home from their very eventful trip to Jamaica where Joan unintentionally ruined Toni's relationship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Say No
programme.desc: Joan continues to suffer in her troubled friendship with Toni and her new relationship with her therapist, Dr. Bales.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Full Court Conspiracy
programme.desc: Torn between her two worlds, Joan struggles with her professional duty while at the same time dealing with the guilt that she is not being true to herself or her beliefs.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Un-Treatable
programme.desc: Maya buys a dress that is too expensive knowing it might cause problems with her husband, Darnell. Joan treats herself to a trip to New York to visit her boyfriend, realizing the distance between the two of them is a problem.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Buh-Bye
programme.desc: Joan ends the friendship between her and Toni after finding out that Toni has been discussing her personal life with co-workers behind her back. Meanwhile, Lynn is trying to cope with her new living situation with Vosco, and his lack of a job.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He
programme.desc: Yvonne is concerned that William is never going to marry her because they have not set a date for the wedding. When Yvonne asks Joan for advice, Joan tries her best to stay out of it but ends up telling Yvonne to move in with William first to try it out.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Joan’s Birthday Suit
programme.desc: For her thirtieth birthday, Joan finds that she is happy with who she is, yet finds that she’s not so happy that her father is a nudist.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Trick or Truth?
programme.desc: When Toni loses her friend Joan, her man, and her gold card, it’s up to Maya and Lynn to help her get her life back together.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Maya Takes a Stan
programme.desc: Due to marital tensions at home, an emotionally fragile Maya finds herself falling into a fantasy affair with a handsome and empathetic new friend, Stan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Mom’s the Word
programme.desc: Toni meets a great man but is dumped after she’s a bad influence on his eight-year-old daughter. Joan dates a movie star but is dumped when he begins to think she’s stalking him.

programme.title: Girlfriends: You Better Watch Out
programme.desc: Maya refuses to end her relationship with Stan, despite her girlfriends' repeated warnings to do so.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Have a Dream House
programme.desc: As a gesture of his love, William buys a beautiful house for his fiance, Yvonne.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Sister, Sistah
programme.desc: Lynn's Caucasian sister, Tanya, travels to LA to help Lynn celebrate her Gotcha Day: the day when Lynn's childhood adoption to her Caucasian family became legal.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter
programme.desc: The week of William and Yvonne's wedding is finally here and all goes smoothly until Yvonne discovers William never told his mother about Yvonne...ever.
programme.sub-title: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter

programme.title: Girlfriends: Can’t Stan Ya
programme.desc: After a horrid kiss, Maya finally realizes she must end her relationship with Stan. Afterward, however, Stan deviously becomes good friends with Maya’s husband, just to make Maya squirm as he says he may or may not tell her husband about them.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Take Me Out After the Ball Game
programme.desc: As the company softball season kicks off, Joan falls for her new teammate: a young new employee from the mailroom; however, things go sour when she publicly dismisses their relationship due to their age difference.
programme.sub-title: Take Me Out After the Ball Game

programme.title: Girlfriends: Child’s in Charge
programme.desc: Toni decides to make something of her life by opening her own exclusive real estate agency after she sells a four-point-two million-dollar property.

programme.title: Girlfriends: X Does Not Mark the Spot
programme.desc: Joan becomes more adventurous with her boyfriend Chris, but inadvertently takes Ecstasy at a club with him and has a bad trip that puts her into the hospital.
programme.sub-title: X Does Not Mark the Spot

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Taming of the Realtess
programme.desc: Toni is frantic as the grand opening of her real estate agency is less than a week away.

programme.title: Girlfriends: My Mother, Myself
programme.desc: Just before Mother’s Day, Maya is disturbed when her mother announces she’s selling the house and moving to San Bernardino with her boyfriend. Toni has issues when her mother shows up unannounced for Mother’s Day and continually embarrasses her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Dessert
programme.desc: Unable to work out living with Joan, Lynn moves in with William and the two hit it off in more ways than one; becoming casual, unattached sex buddies.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Into the Woods
programme.desc: Joan dumps Chris when she learns her ex-boyfriend, Sean, is moving back to town; but is crushed when Sean lies about being engaged just to avoid being in a relationship with her again.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Join Christiane Amanpour as she provides her powerful interview skills and provocative analysis on global stories that matter to you.

programme.title: Isa Soares Tonight
programme.desc: Isa Soares breaks down the news of the day, curating the stories that matter as well as getting into exactly why they are important to discuss.

programme.title: What We Know With Max Foster
programme.desc: Max Foster brings a definitive round-up of the day's top stories, giving you the urgent need-to-know context and diving deeper into the questions sparking debate around the world.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest presents a round-up of the day's global business news with a focus on international business and market trends.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: Covering all the day's top stories around the country and the globe, from politics to money, sports to popular culture.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Daily sports show features major stories and issues making news in the world of sports.

programme.title: The Brief With Jim Sciutto
programme.desc: The best of CNN's reporting from correspondents across the world, to guide global audiences through the fast-paced news cycle with insight, analysis and hard-hitting interviews.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: Anchor Erin Burnett presents information beyond the regular headlines by providing objective reports on top issues along with compelling interviews, lively debates and in-depth analysis of situations.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Anderson Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: The Source With Kaitlan Collins
programme.desc: Kaitlan Collins chases the facts, asks the tough questions and connects with her sources on prime time to break down the day's biggest news stories.

programme.title: CNN NewsNight With Abby Phillip
programme.desc: Abby Phillip delivers a smart, sharp approach to the day's biggest stories.

programme.title: Laura Coates Live
programme.desc: Laura Coates discusses the daily intersections of politics, pop culture and the law.

programme.title: The Story Is With Elex Michaelson
programme.desc: Elex Michaelson tackles the breaking stories making headlines with sharp analysis, fresh perspective, and a dose of humour.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom
programme.desc: A rotating cast of anchors and contributors present daily headlines and breaking stories live from CNN headquarters in Atlanta.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom
programme.desc: A rotating cast of anchors and contributors present daily headlines and breaking stories live from CNN headquarters in Atlanta.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Daily sports show features major stories and issues making news in the world of sports.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Join Christiane Amanpour as she provides her powerful interview skills and provocative analysis on global stories that matter to you.

programme.title: Early Start With Rahel Solomon
programme.desc: The news you need, first thing. Rahel Solomon has you covered with the latest headlines.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Murdock goes under cover to investigate an assassination plot.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: A girl runs away with her father's top-secret documents.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team uncovers a plot to assassinate an attorney general.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2. The A-Team travels down to Mexico to locate a journalist captured by bandits.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Hannibal and the A-Team rescue a young girl held captive by a violent religious cult.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Hannibal and his men get together to break B.A.'s friend out of a southern jail, where prisoners are forced to fight to the death.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team exposes a group of police officers who are making money as hired killers.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The attempts of a vicious motorcycle gang to over-run and destroy a small western town in order to get their leader out of jail are thwarted by the A-Team.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The mob places a reward of $100,000 for Hannibal's capture after he and the A-Team outwit organized crime chieftain Gianni Christian in a Las Vegas kidnapping.
programme.sub-title: The Rabbit Who Ate Las Vegas

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team comes to the aid of a group of New York shopkeepers who are being harassed by neighborhood protection racketeers.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team crash lands in the backwoods, where they battle a band of crazed mountain men who are about to burn a man at the stake.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team helps a victimized farmer and his daughter deliver their produce to market, saving their farm from bankruptcy.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team rescues an Army General and his daughter from a band of guerilla terrorists.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team rescues a Texas heiress from an arranged marriage with her father's murderer.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Hannibal, Murdock, Face, and B.A. sneak aboard a 747 to rescue its passengers from hijackers.
programme.sub-title: The Beast From the Belly of the Boeing

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: A friend's funeral turns into a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team go in search of a hidden diamond mine in the African jungle, while transporting a dangerous cargo of dynamite.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: A chance meeting in a posh Los Angeles restaurant and a glimpse at a familiar face leads the A-Team on an adventure to save the life of the Vietnamese cook who helped them escape from the prisoner of war camp in Vietnam.

programme.title: 1999 Farmers Insurance Open
programme.desc: Tiger Woods started the weekend 9 strokes off the pace, but earned a 2-shot victory over Billy Ray Brown.

programme.title: 2004 Farmers Insurance Open
programme.desc: Watch as John Daly prevails in a three-man playoff to capture his fifth PGA TOUR title, his first in nearly nine years, at the 2004 Farmers Insurance Open.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: Ben Griffin's 2025 PGA TOUR season changed his life. Learn how in this documentary.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes with Neal Shipley as he prepares for his rookie season on the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: 2008 Farmers Insurance Open
programme.desc: Tiger Woods successfully defended his title for the 3rd consecutive year at the Farmers Insurance Open.

programme.title: PGA TOUR - The Drop
programme.desc: Billy Kratzert joins the show to discuss Brooks Koepka's return to the PGA TOUR at the Farmers Insurance Open.

programme.title: The CUT
programme.desc: Brooks Koepka returns to the PGA TOUR, Scottie Scheffler opens his season with a win at The American Express.

programme.title: Scottie Scheffler Wins the 2026 American Express
programme.desc: Scottie Scheffler becomes the second fastest to register 20 TOUR wins in La Quinta, Calif.

programme.title: 2023 Farmers Insurance Open - Saturday
programme.desc: The final round of the 2023 Farmers Insurance Open, where Max Homa won his sixth PGA TOUR title. Homa fired a 6-under 66 to overcome a five-shot deficit entering the day and win by two.

programme.title: 2025 Farmers Insurance Open - Final Round
programme.desc: Watch the final round of the 2025 Farmers Insurance Open from Torrey Pines Golf Course in San Diego.

programme.title: The Starter
programme.desc: Catch up on all the latest happenings on the PGA TOUR with feature content on the best players in the world.

programme.title: PGA TOUR Backspin
programme.desc: Watch the latest highlights from the best players in the world from courses around the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: Garrick Higgo Wins the Corales Puntacana Championship
programme.desc: Garrick Higgo battled back during a windy final round as the South African captured his 2nd PGA TOUR victory.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On the show are Hollywood great Tom Hanks starring in `Sully', Gemma Arterton playing Joan of Arc in `Saint Joan', Joseph Gordon-Levitt playing the NSA whistle-blower in `Snowden', the UK's greatest distance runner Mo Farah, and music from Olly Murs.
programme.sub-title: Tom Hanks, Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Mo Farah, Gemma Arterton and Olly Murs

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa are `Game of Thrones' star Emilia Clarke, pop icon Gloria Estefan, star of `Broadchurch' David Tennant, and the irrepressible Miriam Margolyes. Also, Leon Bridges performs the new single `Beyond'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa are Hollywood legend Meryl Streep, British actress Carey Mulligan, co-starring in new British drama `Suffragette', actress Nicole Kidman, domestic goddess Nigella Lawson, and music from rising British star Gabrielle Aplin.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa tonight: pop legend Sir Elton John, chatting and performing `Voyeur'; actress Dame Judi Dench, starring in `Philomena'; journalist and presenter Jeremy Paxman; and comedian John Bishop.
programme.sub-title: Judi Dench, Jeremy Paxman, John Bishop, Elton John

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa this time are Melissa McCarthy, Kristen Wiig, Kate McKinnon, Leslie Jones and Charlie Sheen. With music from Christine and the Queens, performing `Tilted'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham are the mighty Arnold Schwarzenegger, `Game of Thrones' actress Emilia Clarke, Jake Gyllenhaal, Cara Delevingne and Tinie Tempah.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa are Oscar-winner Anne Hathaway, actresses Rebel Wilson and Jodie Comer, and actor Daniel Radcliffe. With music from Mabel, who performs her single `Don't Call Me Up'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa this time are `Great British Bake-Off' star Paul Hollywood, actor and writer Dame Joan Collins, and `Cinderella' co-stars Lily James and Richard Madden. Plus, music from DNCE, performing `Cake By the Ocean'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on Graham's sofa are Benedict Cumberbatch, now appearing in `Doctor Strange', Eddie Redmayne, starring in the Harry Potter prequel `Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them', Bryan Cranston and chat and music from LeAnn Rimes.
programme.sub-title: Benedict Cumberbatch, Eddie Redmayne, Bryan Cranston and LeAnn Rimes

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa: `Hulk' star Mark Ruffalo, `Britain's Got Talent' judge Amanda Holden, Michael Sheen promoting Soccer Aid, and music from Ed Sheeran.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Guests include Margot Robbie and Allison Janney, both nominated at the Oscars for their roles in true-life drama `I, Tonya', Daniel Kaluuya, starring in Marvel film `Black Panther', and Alicia Vikander. Plus, Camila Cabello performs `Havana'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa tonight: Eurovision winner Conchita Wurst; comedy favourite Dawn French, talking about touring solo; Hollywood star Kirsten Dunst, starring in new movie `The Two Faces of January'; Bear Grylls; and Sam Smith.
programme.sub-title: Kirsten Dunst, Bear Grylls, Dawn French, Conchita Wurst, Sam Smith

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa are Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley, and Rebel Wilson, with music by Iggy Pop, who performs `Sunday'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Colin Farrell, Rachel Weisz, Dawn French, Chris O'Dowd join Graham on the sofa. With a performance from Rod Stewart.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are Goldie Hawn and Amy Schumer together in action comedy `Snatched', Hollywood star Orlando Bloom, and John Boyega appearing in `Woyzeck' at the Old Vic. Plus, Lucie Jones performs the UK's Eurovision entry `Never Give Up on You'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are Liam Payne who chats and sings `Strip That Down', Salma Hayek in `Beatriz at Dinner', comedian and writer David Walliams talking about `The World's Worst Children 2', and the stars of `White Gold' Ed Westwick and James Buckley.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on Graham's sofa: everyone's favourite hot vicar from `Fleabag' Andrew Scott, Stephen Fry on tour with `Mythos: A Trilogy', singer-turned-actor Paloma Faith, and top comic Lee Mack. With music from Bastille, who perform `Joy'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined on the sofa by actor Zac Efron, comic actor Seth Rogen and `Friends' star Matt LeBlanc.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: James Bond star Daniel Craig talks about the new Bond film `Spectre'. He's joined by Naomie Harris who plays Eve Moneypenny, and pop superstar Sam Smith, who chats and performs `Writing's On The Wall' in a live world exclusive.
programme.sub-title: Daniel Craig, Naomie Harris, Christoph Waltz, Sam Smith

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham Norton is joined by Judi Dench and Kenneth Branagh, who star in the new movie version of `Murder on the Orient Express', Johnny Depp, Michelle Pfeiffer and Josh Gad. Plus music from St Vincent, who performs the single `Los Ageless'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Tom Cruise and Annabelle Wallis, starring in `The Mummy', and Zac Efron, promoting the movie revival of `Baywatch'. Plus, singer-songwriter Beth Ditto performs her single `Fire'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on Graham's sofa, music icon Madonna talking about her album `Madame X', acting legend Sir Ian McKellen talking about touring, director Danny Boyle, and actors Lily James and Himesh Patel. With music from Sheryl Crow.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa this time are `Frasier' star Kelsey Grammer, comic actor Tamsin Greig, Oscar-nominated Salma Hayek and singer-songwriter Alicia Keys.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Guests include Hollywood megastar Tom Cruise, his `Mission: Impossible 6' co-stars Henry Cavill and Rebecca Ferguson, and British actor and writer Simon Pegg. Plus, music and chat from Paloma Faith, who performs `Til I'm Done'.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Pandora's Box
programme.desc: While Boone is haunted by hallucinations of captain Lucas Johnson, the Taelon Rho'ha is to undergo a experiment of experiencing human emotions.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: If You Could Read my Mind
programme.desc: The Taelon Commonality is invaded and psychic Katya Petrenko is the prime suspect. She tells Boone she knows about his secret activities and needs him.
programme.sub-title: If You Could Read my Mind

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Wrath of Achilles
programme.desc: The resistance releases a virus of Augur on the Taelon headquarters in Washington. Things get out of hand.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Devil You Know
programme.desc: While a dying colonel implants his brain in a deceased criminal, the Taelons show a new electromagnetic pulse weapon. Jonathan Doors wishes to acquire it.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Law and Order
programme.desc: The Synod agrees to let Rho'ha be charged with murder in a human court. He will be prosecuted by Joshua Doors, the son of Jonathan Doors.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: A new inter-dimensional portal system for human travel turns out to be something more when the resistance discover a CVI in a boy who got lost in the system.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Infection
programme.desc: Ne'eg is killed by a bacteria that is both deadly to Taelons and Humans. If it spreads, it might be disastrous. A new racist group attacks a community center.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Destruction
programme.desc: The Liberation sends a team to destroy the scarecrow probe before the Taelons crack it. Joshua Doors is appointed as secretary of Human-Taelon relations.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Joining
programme.desc: An excavation team unleashes a very dangerous Taelon on Earth. Siobhan Beckett comes over to America. She suspects Lili of being a spy and investigates.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: First of its Kind
programme.desc: Following Boone's confrontation with Hegel that left him severely injured, he was eliminated by Zo'or. Beckett who was impregnated by Hegel gives birth to a boy who immediately grows up to a man.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Avatus
programme.desc: Da'an looses his contact with the the Taelon Commonality. This causes him to change and Zo'or instructs that Da'an must be killed; because he's now considered a threat.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: An exhibition for the first arrival of the Taelons showcases different artifacts. An artifact causes Liam to travel to an alternate time. Zo'or questions how Da'an was able to rejoin the Commonality.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Dimensions
programme.desc: Liam and Augur are in a Taelon shuttle craft, when something happens they get thrown into a parallel universe. They discover that humanity is at war with the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Moonscape
programme.desc: Augur is forced to take part in a Taelon experiment. Da'an and Liam try to convince Zo'or that altering minds and implanting humans to make them aggressive to fight the Jaridians is not the correct path. Maiya makes contact with Sandoval.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Sleepers
programme.desc: A Jaridian probe lands on Earth and begins sucking energy from the Comonality. The Taelons use humans in a near-death state to keep their connection while Lili and Sandoval try to find out what is draining the energy.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Fissures
programme.desc: When large scale blackouts begin to occur in cities with portals, Taelon technology is blamed. Augur and Liam investigate the death of a portal worker by a strange creature that feeds on energy and is particularly attracted to the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Redemption
programme.desc: Siobhan falls while rock climbing. As the others search for her, she dreams that she is on trial for supporting the Taelons. Zo'or questions whether Sandoval's CVI imperative to serve the Taelons has broken down as others are experiencing.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Between Heaven and Hell
programme.desc: Dr. Belman's daughter does human testing on herself with an accelerant that causes her to rapidly evolve. Joshua Doors decides somebody should run against President Thompson in the election, somebody who will stand up to the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Isabel
programme.desc: Maiya experiences dimensional phasing. Augur and Liam believe they need to bring Isabel and Maiya together to let the universe merge them. Jonathan Doors announces his presidential candidacy and meets with Zo'or.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict
programme.desc: An escaped Jiridian takes Lili hostage. Their shuttle is sucked on to a Kimera ship where they, with Liam, have to go through an obstacle course to get to the core to exit.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict
programme.desc: Using Jiridian technology, scientists create a physical bio surrogates where a human consciousness can be transferred. When a surrogate escapes, they must find it before it uses its fighting and weapons knowledge.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Second Chances
programme.desc: A Taelon program offers a transformation into a younger, healthier version of oneself. Liam fears that something more dangerous is also happening. Lili is worried that Doors' run for the presidency is negatively affecting the resistance.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's day four and James Braxton and Charles Hanson are heading for Cornwall, where Charles has an enchanting experience and James goes to the fair.
programme.sub-title: James Braxton and Charles Hanson, Day 4

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Charles Hanson head deep into Cornwall on the last day of their road trip. James goes to church before the final auction in Truro.
programme.sub-title: James Braxton and Charles Hanson, Day 5

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's a new road trip! Anita Manning and Philip Serrell are touring the north of England in their classic car, aiming for a profit in Edinburgh.
programme.sub-title: Anita Manning and Philip Serrel, Day 1

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: As Anita Manning and Philip Serrell head to auction in County Durham, Philip develops an odd interest in sewing and Anita eyes up a wedding dress.
programme.sub-title: Anita Manning and Philip Serrel, Day 2
